params.name: Huaqiang's Motorcycle
params.description: A man was selling melons by the roadside. Hua Qiang rode up on his motorcycle and asked, "How much per pound?" The vendor quoted a price as if it were gold, explaining that these were greenhouse melons. Hua Qiang inquired if they were guaranteed to be ripe. Realizing he was being nitpicked, the vendor hung a huge magnet on the weighing scale. Hua Qiang split a melon to show the vendor what's what. A buddy named Sa Ri Lang watched as Hua Qiang rode away coolly on his motorcycle, skr.
